summary.business_type: B2B SaaS / Developer Tool (AI agent infrastructure)
top_findings: Zero structured data (JSON-LD) anywhere on betteragent.dev or docs.betteragent.dev
Identical <title> and meta description on 6+ distinct pages (home, pricing, demo, contact, sign-in, sign-up)
No sitemap.xml on either the marketing site or the docs subdomain (both 404)
robots.txt explicitly blocks GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, meta-externalagent — ironic and self-defeating for a product selling AI agent infrastructure
/demo renders almost no server-side content (50 characters of text) — effectively invisible to crawlers and an empty social-share target
quick_wins: Write unique title + meta description per page (10 minutes, highest leverage fix available)
Add a sitemap.xml (trivial in Next.js App Router via app/sitemap.ts) and reference it in robots.txt
Add Open Graph + Twitter Card tags (currently completely absent — shared links have no preview)
Add canonical tags site-wide (currently absent everywhere)
Add Organization + SoftwareApplication JSON-LD to the homepage

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: robots.txt present and explicitly allows general crawling (Allow: / for User-agent: *)
Pages are server-prerendered (x-nextjs-prerender header) and served from Vercel/Cloudflare edge cache

findings.title: No sitemap.xml on apex domain or docs subdomain
findings.description: https://betteragent.dev/sitemap.xml and https://docs.betteragent.dev/sitemap.xml both return 404 (soft 404s rendering the site's not-found page). robots.txt also contains no Sitemap: directive.
findings.recommendation: Add app/sitemap.ts (Next.js App Router) listing all indexable routes for both betteragent.dev and docs.betteragent.dev, and add a Sitemap: line to each robots.txt.

findings.title: No canonical tags on any page
findings.description: Checked all 9 crawled pages (home, pricing, demo, contact, privacy, terms, auth/sign-in, auth/sign-up, docs home) — none contain <link rel="canonical">.
findings.recommendation: Add canonical URLs via Next.js metadata API (alternates.canonical) on every page, especially important since www and apex both resolve (307 redirect from betteragent.dev to www.betteragent.dev was observed).

findings.title: AI/LLM crawlers blocked in robots.txt
findings.description: robots.txt disallows GPTBot, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, CCBot, Bytespider, meta-externalagent, Amazonbot, and CloudflareBrowserRenderingCrawler. Content-Signal is set to ai-train=no. This is likely a Cloudflare-managed default, but it blocks the product from appearing in ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI Overview answers — a significant channel for a developer tool audience that researches via AI assistants.
findings.recommendation: Decide deliberately: if the goal is visibility in AI-assisted developer search (highly likely for this product category), allow ai-input access for ClaudeBot/GPTBot/Google-Extended at minimum, even if ai-train stays restricted. See AI Search Readiness section.

findings.title: Missing security response headers
findings.description: No Content-Security-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, or Permissions-Policy headers observed on the homepage response.
findings.recommendation: Add baseline security headers via next.config headers() or vercel.ts — improves trust signals and is increasingly checked by security-aware enterprise buyers evaluating a SaaS vendor.

findings.title: Apex domain redirects to www with a 307 (temporary) redirect
findings.description: betteragent.dev/ returns a 307 to www.betteragent.dev/. 307s are technically fine for SEO (treated as permanent enough by Google when consistent) but a 301 is the conventional choice for a permanent canonical host decision.
findings.recommendation: Switch the apex→www redirect to a 301/308 to remove any ambiguity for crawlers.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Homepage copy is concise, specific, and avoids generic SaaS filler ('Point BetterAgent at your codebase. It reads your routes and server actions...')
Pricing page clearly explains the credit-based billing model with a comparison table

findings.title: /demo page is server-side empty
findings.description: Stripping scripts/styles/tags from the rendered HTML leaves only 50 characters of text on /demo, versus 6,736 on the homepage. The page is fully client-rendered with no SSR fallback content, meta description duplicated from the homepage, and no <h1>.
findings.recommendation: Server-render at least a title, description, and static preview/screenshot on /demo so it has indexable content and a meaningful social/AI-search snippet, even if the interactive demo itself is client-side.

findings.title: No E-E-A-T / authority signals: no About, team, case studies, or testimonials page
findings.description: Full link crawl across all 9 pages turned up only: /, /pricing, /demo, /contact, /privacy, /terms, /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up, and docs pages. There is no page establishing who built the product, customer proof, or usage evidence.
findings.recommendation: Add a lightweight About/company page and at least one customer quote or usage stat on the homepage — both for human trust and because LLM answer engines weight authority/citation signals heavily for B2B tool recommendations.

findings.title: No blog or educational content
findings.description: No /blog route exists (404). For a dev-tool category that competes on discovery via search and AI assistants, there's no long-tail content surface (e.g. 'how to add an AI agent to a Next.js app', integration guides, comparison posts).
findings.recommendation: Not urgent for MVP stage, but flag as a growth lever once core product-market fit is established — docs.betteragent.dev structure could be extended with guides.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Single <h1> present on most pages (home, pricing, contact, privacy, terms, docs, sign-in, sign-up)

findings.title: Duplicate title tag and meta description across 6 distinct pages
findings.description: Home, pricing, demo, contact, auth/sign-in, and auth/sign-up all serve the exact same <title>BetterAgent — the agent layer your SaaS is missing</title> and identical meta description. Only /privacy, /terms, and the docs subdomain have unique titles.
findings.recommendation: Set per-route metadata via Next.js generateMetadata for /pricing, /demo, /contact, and the auth pages. Example: /pricing → 'Pricing — BetterAgent', description summarizing the credit model.

findings.title: No Open Graph or Twitter Card tags anywhere
findings.description: Searched all crawled pages for og: and twitter: meta tags — none found. Links shared in Slack, Twitter/X, or LinkedIn will render with no title, no description, and no image.
findings.recommendation: Add og:title, og:description, og:image (1200x630), twitter:card=summary_large_image via the Next.js metadata API, ideally with a per-page OG image.

findings.title: /demo has no <h1>
findings.description: Consistent with the empty-content finding above — there is no heading structure to crawl.
findings.recommendation: Resolved by the same fix as the Content Quality finding: server-render a heading and supporting copy.

findings.title: No JSON-LD structured data on any page
findings.description: Searched all 9 crawled pages for application/ld+json — zero matches. No Organization, SoftwareApplication, Product, FAQPage, or BreadcrumbList schema anywhere.
findings.recommendation: Add Organization schema sitewide (name, logo, url, sameAs), SoftwareApplication or Product schema with pricing on /pricing (matches the existing comparison table — also makes the plans eligible for rich pricing snippets), and BreadcrumbList on docs pages.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Pages are pre-rendered at build/edge time (x-nextjs-prerender: 1) and served from Vercel/Cloudflare cache (cf-cache-status, x-vercel-cache: HIT)

findings.title: Lab Core Web Vitals data unavailable for this audit
findings.description: PageSpeed Insights API returned a rate-limit error and CrUX API requires an API key not configured in this environment — no Lighthouse or field CWV scores could be captured. Score above is a heuristic estimate based on response headers and architecture (Next.js + Vercel edge + Cloudflare), not a measured Lighthouse score.
findings.recommendation: Re-run with a Google API key (PageSpeed Insights + CrUX) to get real LCP/INP/CLS numbers, especially for the homepage which loads 16 JS chunks.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: Content-Signal header in robots.txt explicitly grants search=yes, meaning traditional search indexing is allowed

findings.title: Major AI crawlers and answer-engine bots are blocked
findings.description: robots.txt disallows GPTBot (ChatGPT), ClaudeBot (Claude), Google-Extended (Gemini/AI Overviews), Applebot-Extended (Apple Intelligence), CCBot (Common Crawl, used to train/ground many models), Bytespider (TikTok/ByteDance), and meta-externalagent (Meta AI). For a product whose entire pitch is 'the agent layer your SaaS is missing,' being invisible to AI assistants when developers ask 'what should I use to add an agent to my SaaS' is a direct hit to the target acquisition channel.
findings.recommendation: This block appears to be Cloudflare's default AI-crawler managed ruleset rather than an intentional product decision — confirm with the team whether blocking AI training was a deliberate IP-protection choice. If discovery matters more than training-data protection, allow ai-input for at least ClaudeBot, GPTBot, and Google-Extended while keeping ai-train=no.

findings.title: No llms.txt file
findings.description: No /llms.txt found at either betteragent.dev or docs.betteragent.dev. This emerging convention helps AI agents/crawlers quickly understand site structure and find canonical documentation.
findings.recommendation: Add an llms.txt pointing to the docs subdomain's quickstart, CLI reference, tools, and components pages — low effort, directly relevant since the docs already have clean MDX content.

categories.name: Images
what_works: No broken or oversized raster images detected — the site uses an icon/SVG-driven design with essentially zero tags on marketing pages

findings.title: No social preview image (og:image) anywhere
findings.description: Consistent with the missing Open Graph findings — there is no image asset configured for social/AI link previews on any page.
findings.recommendation: Create one branded 1200x630 OG image for the homepage at minimum, ideally per-route (especially /pricing) since this is the cheapest visual trust signal for shared links.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Write unique <title> and meta description for /pricing, /demo, /contact, /auth/sign-in, /auth/sign-up
Add app/sitemap.ts for betteragent.dev and docs.betteragent.dev, reference both in robots.txt
Server-render real content (heading + description) on /demo instead of a client-only empty shell
Decide and fix the AI-crawler block in robots.txt (allow ClaudeBot/GPTBot/Google-Extended for ai-input at minimum)

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Add canonical tags sitewide via Next.js metadata API
Add Open Graph + Twitter Card tags with a branded OG image
Add Organization + SoftwareApplication/Product JSON-LD (pricing tiers already exist — map directly to schema)
Add an llms.txt pointing into docs.betteragent.dev

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Add an About/company page with founder or team info
Add at least one customer testimonial or usage stat to the homepage
Add baseline security headers (CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy)

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Re-run PageSpeed Insights / CrUX with an API key to get real Core Web Vitals once traffic volume supports CrUX field data
Consider a docs-extension blog/guides section for long-tail and AI-citation surface area once core product priorities allow
